Interest in Mana Wine's bonded warehouse rises due to impending 200% tariff, offering secure storage and tax deferral for foreign alcohol imports.
news.summary: In Jersey City, New Jersey, importers are seeking refuge at Mana Wine's bonded warehouse as they face a staggering 200% tariff on foreign alcohol imports. This facility provides a secure storage solution for imported goods and offers tax deferral benefits to help navigate the financial impact of the tariff. This move comes amidst rising tensions in the trade industry and concerns over escalating tariffs.

Fed's Goolsbee: Imports are only 11% of GDP, so one time tariffs that are not followed by retaliation are more likely to be transitory.
Fed's Goolsbee: The bigger they are and the more like supply shocks they are the harder it will be for the Fed to look through them.

Bitcoin is a strong buy now, says Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan, calling it the best time in history due to political developments.
news.summary: Matt Hougan, Chief Investment Officer at Bitwise, believes Bitcoin (BTC) is primed for the future due to various political factors. During the Blockworks Digital Asset Summit in New York, he highlighted that current macroeconomic conditions are strengthening Bitcoin's long-term investment outlook but exerting short-term price pressures. Hougan stated that this is the most optimal time in history, considering risk factors, to purchase Bitcoin.

Accelerate Diagnostics reports Q4 net sales of $2.8 million, gross margin at 16%. FY net sales at $11.7 million with $50.0 million net loss.
news.summary: Accelerate Diagnostics, Inc. reported their financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. In the fourth quarter, net sales were $2.8 million, with a net loss of $9.6 million, resulting in a $0.38 net loss per share. They ended the quarter with $16.3 million in cash and cash equivalents, down from $20.9 million at the start. For the full year, net sales were $11.7 million, with a gross margin of 23% and a net loss of $50.0 million, equating to a $2.15 net loss per share. SG&A costs were $21.3 million for the year, down from $31.2 million in the prior year. R&D costs were $16.7 million for the year, lower than the $25.4 million in the prior year. The company plans not to host regular earnings conference calls but will provide financial updates through SEC filings and investor relations materials on their website. The full financial results for 2024 will be filed with the SEC. Non-GAAP financial measures have been used in this press release to evaluate operating performance and trends.

Silexion Therapeutics completes expanded development plan for SIL204, to be unveiled at 2025 NeauxCancer Conference.
news.summary: Silexion Therapeutics will unveil an advanced development strategy for SIL204 at the prominent 2025 NeauxCancer Conference, building upon the groundbreaking preclinical findings from Orthotopic Models. Details of the innovative plan will be shared shortly with the presentation, showcasing the company's progress and vision for the future. The event aims to highlight the potential impact of SIL204 in the field of cancer research and treatment, reflecting the commitment to advancing medical solutions.

news.headline: FDA approves Johnson & Johnson's Tremfya for Crohn's disease, offering IL-23 inhibitor for 1300+ patients with CD.
news.summary: Johnson & Johnson's Tremfya has been granted approval by the FDA for the treatment of adults with moderately to severely active Crohn’s disease. This marks the first IL-23 inhibitor to offer both subcutaneous and intravenous induction options. The approval follows successful results from Phase 3 trials involving over 1,300 patients who did not respond well to conventional treatment or biologics. This development signifies a significant advancement in the management of CD. For more information, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: UK PM's Spokesman: UK military planners will meet next week to look closer at details and structure of the force.
UK PM's Spokesman: Next week the government will accelerate planning for peacekeeping force for Ukraine.
Gold nears $3,100 amid US repatriation for tariff fears and gold audit prospects, surpassing $3,000, fueled by uncertainty.
news.summary: Gold prices have been steadily rising, prompting speculation from the World Gold Council and experts that the U.S. may be bringing back gold reserves due to concerns over tariffs, as well as a desire to bolster domestic reserves, potentially conduct gold audits, and move away from globalization. Recently, gold crossed the $3,000 per ounce milestone and is now on track to reach $3,100, surpassing its previous record of $3,057.51.Josh Phair, CEO of Scottsdale Mint, noted a noticeable uptick in gold repatriation starting in December, evidenced by an increase in exchange for physical premiums. Phair attributed this trend to banks avoiding risks associated with tariffs, stating that the intention was to relocate all gold reserves to the U.S. to mitigate potential economic uncertainties. He also mentioned concerns about the new administration's policies and the possibility of a gold audit following suggestions from prominent figures like Elon Musk and Donald Trump. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Spokesman: Ukraine and US teams continue dialogue regarding the deal.
Elon Musk advises Tesla employees to keep their stock as it triples to $1,500.
Pulmatrix to divest assets in merger with Cullgen, including Phase 2-ready migraine drug; year-end 2024: Revenues $7.8M, cash balance $9.5M.
news.summary: Pulmatrix, a biopharmaceutical company, announced a merger with Cullgen expected to close in the first half of 2025. They plan to divest assets, including a Phase 2-ready acute migraine candidate, PUR3100. Pulmatrix also shared financial results for 2024, noting a $7.8 million revenue with decreased R&D expenses at $7.2 million. They reported a $2.6 million loss on transactions with MannKind Corporation and held $9.5 million in cash as of December 31, 2024. Their iSPERSE™ technology features approximately 149 patents. The company foresees the cash position enough to fund operations until the merger with Cullgen closes.

news.headline: Crypto market down on March 21 with $2.75 trillion market cap, large liquidations, and bearish sentiment amid US equities plunge.
news.summary: On March 21, the cryptocurrency market saw a 2.5% drop, totaling a market capitalization of $2.75 trillion. Factors leading to the decline included over $230 million wiped off the market, with Bitcoin failing to maintain a rally to $87,000. Trump’s speech at the Digital Asset Summit had little impact. Ether fell below $2,000 and XRP, Solana, and Cardano also saw losses. $235 million in leveraged positions were liquidated in 24 hours, mostly long positions. The market mirrors US equities’ weakness, with S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow Jones all dropping. A bear flag pattern in the market signals potential further decline, with a downside target at $2.23 trillion. Despite this, the Fear & Greed Index sits at 27, reflecting cautious sentiment in the market.

news.headline: Highwood Asset Management Ltd. reports a successful Q4 2024, with production up by 48% to 5,966 boe/d. Adjusted EBITDA at $19.0 million and capital plan for 2025 set at $60-65 million. Borrowing base increased to $120 million in Q4 2024. For detailed info, see Benzinga.com.
news.summary: Highwood Asset Management Ltd. reported its financial and operating results for Q4 2024, showing a 48% increase in average corporate production to 5,966 boe/d from last year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2024 was $19.0 million, with adjusted funds flow of $7.2 million. The company's NAV per share grew by approximately 35% on a PDP BTNPV10 NAV/share basis. Highwood incurred capital expenditures of $11 million in Q4 2024. Their 2025 capital plan is $60-65 million with production guidance of 6,200-6,400 boe/d. Borrowing base increased from $110 million to $120 million due to successful drilling programs. With these positive results, early repayment of a Promissory Note reduced interest expenses. Net debt stood at $97.832 million, with shareholder's equity at $132.087 million and shares outstanding at 15,154. Production levels saw significant increases compared to the previous year, with average realized prices showing slight declines. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Pegasystems Inc. will pay a $0.03 quarterly dividend per share on April 15, 2025, for shareholders as of April 1, 2025.
news.summary: In Waltham, Massachusetts, Pegasystems Inc. (NASDAQ: PEGA) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.03 per share, continuing their current dividend program. The dividend for Q2 2025 is scheduled to be distributed on April 15, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of April 1, 2025. Pega, known as The Enterprise Transformation CompanyTM, aids companies in adapting with enterprise AI decisioning and workflow automation to Build for Change®. The company is recognized globally for its innovative solutions and commitment to transformation.

news.headline: Emma Moore from Aya Locums named “2024 Locum Tenens Provider of the Year” by Locumpedia for excellence in patient care.
news.summary: In San Diego, Emma Moore, a Family Nurse Practitioner (NP) with Aya Locums, has been recognized by Locumpedia as the "2024 Locum Tenens Provider of the Year". This award, now in its third year, highlights the commitment, skill, and influence of locum tenens physicians nationwide. It specifically acknowledges providers like Emma who offer top-notch patient care as required in underserved areas. Emma, formerly from West Virginia, ventured far from home to provide crucial services across the country.

news.summary: The billionaire was scheduled to receive a briefing on the military's confidential strategies regarding China, sparking concerns about his influence and privileges.

news.headline: iECURE presents data at ACMG meeting on first infant dosed with ECUR-506 in OTC-HOPE trial showing partial enzyme activity restoration.
news.summary: In Philadelphia, iECURE, Inc., a gene editing firm concentrating on mutation-agnostic in vivo gene insertion therapies for liver conditions, shared data at the 2025 ACMG Annual Clinical Genetics Meeting on OTC-HOPE Phase 1/2 study of ECUR-506. The findings indicate partial restoration of functional ornithine transcarbamylase enzyme activity in the initial patient dosed, showing promise for treating liver disorders. The study highlights advancements in gene therapy aimed at addressing significant unmet medical needs.

Perfect Corp and Fresh Faces Rx partner to enhance patient care using AI tech. Portland's Fresh Faces Rx to use AI-powered Skincare Pro for consultations.
news.summary: Perfect Corp., a renowned company listed on the NYSE as PERF, specializes in AI and AR beauty and fashion technology. Recently, a collaboration was revealed between Perfect Corp. and Fresh Faces Rx, a top medical spa in Portland. This partnership aims to transform patient consultations and skincare suggestions by leveraging Perfect Corp.'s innovative AI-powered Skincare Pro technology. Fresh Faces Rx is recognized for promoting natural beauty and delivering precise treatments in ethical aesthetics. Through the utilization of advanced AI skin analysis, they are now offering objective and quantifiable skincare recommendations to their clientele.

Sow Good Inc. (Nasdaq: SOWG) reports Q4 2024 results for freeze-dried candy industry leader.
news.summary: Sow Good Inc., a leading company in freeze-dried candy and treat manufacturing headquartered in Irving, Texas, recently disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year concluding on December 31, 2024. The company, listed on Nasdaq as SOWG, shared the results of its operations during this period. The company is known for being a pioneer in the industry.

news.headline: $8.85 million stolen from Ethereum DeFi platform Zoth in second hack this month due to compromised private key.
news.summary: A recent cyberattack, possibly facilitated by an unauthorized access to a private key, has impacted a real-world asset platform for the second time within a month. This security breach has raised concerns about the platform's vulnerability to such attacks.

Power Knot introduces LCD biodigester for cannabis waste in Fremont. A fully enclosed, automated system for efficient disposal.
news.summary: In Fremont, California, on March 21, 2025, a leading company in on-site organic waste management, Power Knot LLC, unveiled the LCD biodigester designed for cannabis waste disposal. This innovative system is fully enclosed and automated, offering a cutting-edge solution for handling cannabis waste.

news.headline: Gundry MD Total Restore supplement sells over 7 million units with 20 pro-digestive ingredients for gut health.
news.summary: The Total Restore supplement by Gundry MD is designed to be the ultimate gut guardian, containing a unique blend of more than 20 pro-digestive ingredients aimed at bolstering a robust and healthy gut lining. The company, based in Los Angeles, revealed this groundbreaking product on March 21, 2025. Gundry MD®, known for its innovative health and wellness supplements, has introduced a formulation packed with essential nutrients to support digestive health and overall well-being.

news.headline: According to Anthony Pompliano, Tether has become "too big to fail" as the largest stablecoin issuer due to its role in the US financial system. Tether is now the seventh-largest buyer of U.S. Treasury bills in 2024.
news.summary: During a recent episode of Anthony Pompliano's show, it was stated that Tether (CRYPTO: USDT), the leading stablecoin issuer globally, has reached a point of being "too big to fail" within the U.S. financial system. Pompliano highlighted that Tether's increasing demand for U.S. Treasuries has solidified its significance in the international financial landscape. In fact, Pompliano stated that Tether has emerged as one of the primary purchasers of U.S. Treasury bills, surpassing several nations to rank as the seventh-largest buyer in 2024. This development underscores Tether's substantial influence and pivotal role in today's markets.

news.headline: Global Packaging Automation Market to reach US$ 52.2 Billion by 2032 with CAGR of 3.7% from 2024 to 2032. Key drivers: demand for enhanced packaging efficiency, rising e-commerce operations, and advanced technologies. Major players like ABB Ltd., Rockwell Automation Inc., and Siemens AG are leading the innovation in robotic, data-driven, and flexible packaging solutions. E-commerce and logistics automation, sustainability focus, technological advancements, and cost efficiency are key trends fueling market growth. Automated solutions streamline packaging tasks, enhance accuracy, and improve workplace safety.
news.summary: The global Packaging Automation Market Size was valued at US$ 37.5 Billion in 2023 and is projected to reach US$ 52.2 Billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 3.7% from 2024 to 2032. Key drivers include increased demand for packaging efficiency, rising e-commerce operations, and advanced technology development. Leading players like ABB Ltd., Rockwell Automation Inc., and Siemens AG are innovating with robotic, data-driven, and flexible packaging solutions. Automation in packaging includes machinery, technology, and robotics to enhance operational efficiency, reduce errors, minimize waste, and optimize resources. Industries like food & beverage, e-commerce, and logistics are increasingly adopting automated packaging systems for cost reduction, productivity increase, and sustainability objectives. Automated solutions reduce labor, improve workplace safety, and enhance speed and precision in packaging operations. E-commerce growth is a key driver, with automated solutions enhancing mail order fulfillment, delivery speed, and operational cost reduction. Sustainability focus leads businesses to invest in automation for waste reduction and efficient resource allocation. Technological advances like robotics, AI, and vision systems are transforming the industry, improving precision, product sorting, and efficiency. Automated packaging solutions significantly reduce labor costs and enhance workplace safety by minimizing manual intervention in tasks like carton sealing and palletizing.

news.headline: Agape Care Group named Top Workplace by USA Today for the second year in a row based on employee feedback survey.
news.summary: Agape Care Group, based in Spartanburg, S.C., has been recognized with a USA Today Top Workplaces Award for the second consecutive year. The company, owned by Ridgemont Equity Partners, specializes in hospice and palliative care. The award, which highlights organizations with exceptional workplace cultures, is based on employee feedback gathered through a survey administered by Energage. Winning this award signifies Agape Care Group's commitment to providing a positive work environment.

news.headline: Dow Jones Futures drop on Quadruple Witching Day, market seeks bullish signal. Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway on a hot streak.
news.summary: Stock market futures experienced a decline as they await a positive indicator. This upcoming Friday marks a quadruple witching day in the market. Berkshire Hathaway, a company led by Warren Buffett, continues to perform exceptionally well. Buffett's conglomerate is on a successful streak and is poised to extend it further. The market seems unsettled, looking for signs of improvement. The Dow Jones Futures reported a drop, reflecting the uncertainty in the market on the quadruple witching day. Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway is emerging as a standout performer during this period.
